title: T-025 — Wiring: BOM snapshot pattern at WO creation (ADR-002)
prompt:
# T-025 — Wiring: BOM snapshot pattern at WO creation (ADR-002)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §7.1, §7.5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Service producing immutable BOM snapshots at WO creation; idempotent per WO+BOM key.

Snapshot JSON shape mirrors API output of T-012 detail. Idempotency check: existing (work_order_id, bom_header_id) snapshot returns existing row. Org-scoped.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- lib/technical/bom/snapshot.ts [create]
- tests/wiring/bom-snapshot.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- WO state machine (08-PRODUCTION)
- Snapshot viewer UI

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given an active BOM with 2 lines + 1 co-product, when createBomSnapshot called, then snapshot_json contains 1 header + 2 lines + 1 co_product.
2. Given snapshot already exists for (WO,BOM) pair, when called again, then no new row written and same row returned.
3. Given itemId without active BOM, when called, then exception 'NO_ACTIVE_BOM' raised.

## Test strategy
- RED tests for shape + idempotency + error path.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/wiring/bom-snapshot.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not allow mutation of an existing snapshot.
- Do not include private_jsonb in snapshot.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
